result:
All fixes are shipped and the build is verified. Here's the summary:

**Root cause**: this SaaS pivoted products (InboxZap, email cleanup → Lumina, AI meeting assistant) and the migration was left half-done, which explains almost every organic-traffic problem in the GSC data.

**What was broken:**
- `<title>`/meta still said "InboxZap" while the homepage content was 100% Lumina
- `/features` and `/pricing` were linked in nav but had no route — real 404s
- `/faq` 301-redirected to `/#faq`, but no FAQ content existed on the homepage — dead end
- The blog fetched from `/api/posts`, which doesn't exist anywhere in the app — permanently broken, zero content for crawlers, no individual post routes at all
- The sitemap advertised the broken `/features`/`/pricing` pages and explicitly excluded blog posts (a `TODO` left in the code)
- All 12 indexed legacy blog posts target old InboxZap keywords (email cleanup, Gmail storage, unsubscribe) — irrelevant to the new product even if fixed
- The branded query "inboxzap" still converts well (position 4.1) but had nowhere to land an explanation

**What I fixed:**
- Rewrote metadata/OG tags to match Lumina, added `SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD and a dynamic OG image
- Built real `/features` and `/pricing` pages
- Added a working FAQ section including a direct "is Lumina the same as InboxZap?" answer to capture that residual branded traffic
- Rebuilt the blog as static server-rendered pages (fixed the broken client fetch), with 4 new posts targeting realistic meeting-AI search intent
- 301-redirected legacy `/blog/post-N` URLs to `/blog` to consolidate link equity instead of leaving 404s
- Fixed the sitemap to dynamically reflect only real, live pages

Verified with `npx next build` — clean compile, all routes including the new static blog posts prerender successfully. Full diagnosis and a prioritized next-steps list are in `ACTION-PLAN.md`. Note: I couldn't reach `http://127.0.0.1:64046` to do a live/CWV check — it accepted connections but never responded, so that's flagged as a follow-up rather than something I could verify.
